# supportsTextEncoding

**Instance Property**

A Boolean that indicates whether the document view supports different text encodings.

## Declaration

```swift
@MainActor var supportsTextEncoding: Bool { get }
```

## Discussion

[doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true] if the receiver’s document view can support different text encodings; otherwise, [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/false].

## Getting and Setting Content Information

- **canShowMIMEType(_:)**: Returns whether the receiver can display content of a given MIME type.
- **mimeTypesShownAsHTML()**: Returns a list of MIME types that WebKit renders as HTML.
- **setMIMETypesShownAsHTML(_:)**: Sets the MIME types that WebKit attempts to render as HTML.
- **canShowMIMEType(asHTML:)**: Returns whether the receiver interprets a MIME type as HTML.
- **customTextEncodingName**: The custom text encoding name.
- **textSizeMultiplier**: The font size multiplier for text displayed in web frame view objects managed by the receiver.
